title: fixed json decoding bug in daemon thread
body: fixed bug in daemon thread that is triggered when trying to convert an empty message_json to dict. Still raising the JSONDecodeError in case decoding fails for any other reason

step_plan.summary: Prevent the ChromiumDriver WebSocket receiver daemon thread from calling `json.loads()` on empty payloads, avoiding spurious `JSONDecodeError` crashes while keeping `JSONDecodeError` behavior for other invalid non-empty JSON messages.

steps.operation: Guard against empty/whitespace WebSocket payloads before JSON-decoding in the receiver daemon loop
steps.reason: `json.loads()` raises `JSONDecodeError` on empty strings (and typically whitespace-only strings). The receiver daemon thread should skip decoding when `_ws.recv()` returns an empty/falsey payload, while preserving the current behavior of raising `JSONDecodeError` for any other non-empty invalid JSON payloads.
steps.side_effects: Prevents background thread crashes caused by empty frames, improving CDP stability. If upstream websocket uses empty payloads to signal closure/keepalive, this change will now silently `continue` (or equivalent) rather than erroring; ensure this doesn’t mask intended shutdown behavior. Existing behavior for malformed non-empty JSON remains (thread may still terminate with `JSONDecodeError`, as required by the PR). Add/adjust any tests that simulate `_ws.recv()` returning `''`, `'   '`, and invalid JSON like `'{'`.
